revision.text:
[[ja:榎神社 (各務原市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Enoki Shrine | native_name = 榎神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Enoki Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.366506|136.830231}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Enoki Shrine'''|榎神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11465292|ltq=Q11465292|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 46 ** P155: {{ill|Ufusunano Shrine|lt=Ufusunano Shrine|qid=Q135039492|1=Ufusunano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11379320|ltq=Q11379320|qid=Q11379320|1=Ifuribe Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Haguri District|lt=Haguri district|en|Haguri District|hu|Haguri körzet|ja|葉栗郡|ko|하구리군 (아이치현)|pl|Powiat Haguri|sr|Област Хагури|zh|葉栗郡|qid=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.366506|136.830231}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181386 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Japan|lt=Japan|ab|Иапониа|ace|Jeupang|ady|Японие|af|Japan|als|Japan|alt|Јопон|ami|Ripon|am|ጃፓን|ang|Iapan|anp|जापान|an|Chapón|arc|ܝܦܢ|ar|اليابان|arnews|تصنيف:اليابان|arquote|اليابان|ary|الجاپون|arz|اليابان|ast|Xapón|as|জাপান|atj|Japan|avk|Nipona|av|Япония|awa|जापान|ay|Nihun|azb|ژاپون|az|Yaponiya|azquote|Yaponiya|ban|Jepang|bar|Japan|bat_smg|Japuonėjė|ba|Япония|bbc|Japang|bcl|Hapon|bdr|Gipun|be_x_old|Японія|be|Японія|bew|Jepang|bg|Япония|bgnews|Категория:Япония|bh|जापान|bi|Japan|bjn|Japang|blk|ဂျပန်ခမ်းထီ|bm|Japan|bn|জাপান|bnquote|জাপান|bnvoyage|জাপান|bo|ཉི་ཧོང་།|bpy|জাপান|br|Japan|bs|Japan|bsnews|Kategorija:Japan|btm|Jepang|bug|ᨍᨛᨄ|bxr|Япон Улас|ca|Japó|canews|Categoria:Japó|caquote|Japó|cbk_zam|Japón|cdo|Nĭk-buōng|ceb|Hapon|ce|Ипнойчоь|chr|ᏣᏩᏂᏏ|ch|Chapan|chy|Japan|ckb|ژاپۆن|co|Giappone|crh|Japon|csb|Japòńskô|cs|Japonsko|csnews|Kategorie:Japonsko|csquote|Japonsko|csvoyage|Japonsko|cu|Ꙗпѡнїꙗ|cv|Япони|cy|Japan|dag|Japan|da|Japan|de|Japan|denews|Kategorie:Japan|dequote|Japan|devoyage|Japan|din|Japan|diq|Japonya|dsb|Japańska|dtp|Jepun|dty|जापान|dv|ޖަޕާނު|dz|ཇ་པཱན|ee|Japan|el|Ιαπωνία|elnews|Κατηγορία:Ιαπωνία|elquote|Ιαπωνία|elvoyage|Ιαπωνία|eml|Giapòṅ|en|Japan|ennews|Category:Japan|enquote|Japan|envoyage|Japan|eo|Japanio|eonews|Kategorio:Japanujo|eoquote|Japanio|eovoyage|Japanio|es|Japón|esnews|Categoría:Japón|esquote|Japón|esvoyage|Japón|et|Jaapan|etquote|Jaapan|eu|Japonia|ext|Japón|fa|ژاپن|fanews|رده:ژاپن|faquote|ژاپن|favoyage|ژاپن|ff|Japan|fiu_vro|Jaapan|fi|Japani|finews|Luokka:Japani|fivoyage|Japani|fj|Japani|fo|Japan|frp|Japon|frr|Jaapan|fr|Japon|frnews|Catégorie:Japon|frquote|Japon|frvoyage|Japon|fur|Gjapon|fy|Japan|gag|Yaponiya|gan|日本|ga|An Мастор|my|ဂျပန်နိုင်ငံ|mzn|جاپون|nap|Giappone|nds_nl|Japan|nds|Japan|ne|जापान|new|जापान|nia|Jepang|nl|Japan|nlnews|Categorie:Japan|nlvoyage|Japan|nn|Japan|nov|Japan|no|Japan|nqo|ߗ߭ߊߔߐ߲߫|nrm|Japon|nr|Japane|nso|Japane|nv|Binááʼádaałtsʼózí Dineʼé Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Kahashimano Shrine|lt=Kahashimano Shrine|qid=Q135039494|1=Kahashimano Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県江南市宮田町四ツ谷', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:榎神社 (各務原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q134893091}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Enoki Shrine |Image = [[File:榎神社 (Kakamigahara City).jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}} Kawashima Matsukura-chō 2433 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (disputed shrine), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate names = |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = October 14 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Enoki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Enoki Shrine''' (えのきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Kawashima Matsukura-chō|Q131870131|qid=Q131870131|1=Kawashima Matsukura-machi}}, {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|qid=Q641491|1=Kakamigahara}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is one of the disputed sites of '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|qid=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1181–1182}}In the “Binō Haguri Kenbunshū,” compiled in the first year of {{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|qid=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}}, the Engishiki Kawashima Shrine is described as Enoki Myōjin of Ushigo Village.. === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown. The former name of this area was Nishiushigo in {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|qid=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Kōnuma-gō|Q11553979|qid=Q11553979|1=Kōnuma-gō}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, and it is said to have been the central area of the islands (Kawashima) of the Kiso River in the vicinity {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1165–1166}}. During the Heian period, it is said to have been the tutelary shrine of a {{ill|lt=Tendai|Q668188|qid=Q668188|1=Tiantai}} temple (later Chōkō-ji)A temple located at 260 Kawashima Matsukura-chō, Kakamigahara City. The Tendai temple converted to {{ill|lt=Jōdo Shinshū|Q912221|qid=Q912221|1=Jōdo Shinshū}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Tempuku (Japan)|Q1194452|qid=Q1194452|lt=Tempuku|1=Tenpuku}} ({{ill|lt=1233|Q5421|qid=Q5421|1=1233}}), and was renamed Chōkō-ji in the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} ({{ill|lt=1577|Q6704|qid=Q6704|1=1577}}). {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1165–1166}}{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1181–1182}}. It was formerly called Shirahige Daimyōjin, but because there was a large {{ill|lt=enoki|Q706339|qid=Q706339|lt=hackberry|1=Celtis sinensis}} tree within the shrine grounds, it was renamed Enoki Myōjin during the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} era by the high-ranking hatamoto {{ill|lt=Tsubouchi clan|Q11425737|qid=Q11425737|1=Tsubouchi clan}} {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1165–1166}}{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1181–1182}}. During the {{ill|lt=Kan’en|Q1145723|qid=Q1145723|1=Kan'en}} era, it was relocated to what later became Kawashima Village, Ōaza Matsukura, Aza Nishiushigo 1979 {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1165–1166}}. In the flood disaster of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}}) {{ill|lt=May 9 (lunar calendar)|Q839631|qid=Q839631|lt=May 9|1=May 9 (Lunisolar)}} (present-day {{ill|lt=June 28|Q2660|qid=Q2660|1=June 28}}), the shrine grounds became a riverbed {{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://yosikitigakubu.g3.xrea.com/saiisi4.html|title=Gifu Prefecture Disaster Records: Data from 1800–1899|publisher=Gifu Prefecture|language=Japanese|accessdate=2025-01-01}}, among other instances of flood damage. Later, because the enshrined location would become the riverbed of a new {{ill|lt=Sanpagawa|Q11356312|qid=Q11356312|lt=main stream|1=main stream}} due to Kiso River improvement works, the shrine was relocated to its current location on April 30, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40) {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1165–1166}}{{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1181–1182}}. It is adjacent to {{ill|lt=Kasekiko Park|Q11554115|qid=Q11554115|1=Kasekiko Park}}, and it is also possible to enter the shrine grounds from the park. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Shinmei Shrine * Konpira Shrine === Other === * A wooden statue (seated figure) is enshrined in the shrine building. Although details of the statue are unknown, it bears the inscription “Kawashima-san” (the {{ill|lt=sangō|Q11058522|qid=Q11058522|lt=mountain name|1=sangō}} of Chōkō-ji) {{sfn|Kawashima Town|1982|pp=1165–1166}}. * The Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines considers the founding to be during the Kan’en era [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=146&shrname=%E2%98%85%E6%A6%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%98%85 Enoki Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines)]. This corresponds to the year the shrine was relocated. === Access === === Transportation === * Get off at “Matsukura Kōminkan-mae” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|qid=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} Kawashima Line; 7-minute walk. * Get off at “Matsukura-guchi” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|qid=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}; 7-minute walk. === References === * {{cite book|Japanese|editor=Kawashima Town|title=History of Kawashima Town: General History Volume|publisher=Kawashima Town|year=1982|ref={{sfnref|Kawashima Town|1982}}}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Enoki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Enoki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kakamigahara, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]
